LANDLORD / VENDOR INFORMATION
NAME: Konza Investments, Inc
VENDOR #: 1126892
CONTACT PERSON: John Trygg
PHONE / EMAIL: (785) 238-4563 no e-mail
ADDRESS: 6131 Old Milford Rd; Milford KS 66514
SUBMITTED BY:
Rhett Mason
DATE:
1/29/2013
COMMENTS:
Paid February rent internally. Schedule regular rent beginning March 1. Send hard copy "Landlord Letter" to address provided. No e-mail.
COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT
THIS COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (the "Lease") made, entered into and executed this 16 day of Sunnay ,2013, by and between
Konza Investments, Inc. a Kansas Corporation (hereinafter called "Lessor") and
The American Bottling Company (hereinafter called "Lessee").
Lessor and Lessee hereby contract and agree as follows:
1. Leased Premises
Lessor hereby leases unto Lessee the land and any improvements located thereon located at 2405 N. Jackson Street, Junction City, KS 66441,
said premises being an approximately 2.3+/- acre tract improved with an approximately 18,580+/- square foot metal building configured as offices and warehouse space (hereafter, "the leased property").
2. Term
The term of this Lease shall be for one (1) year, commencing on February 1, 2013, and expiring at midnight on January 31st, 2014, unless extended as provided for herein ("Initial Term"). This Lease can be extended at the end of the Initial Term for up to two additional one-year terms ("Extended Term") by Lessee giving Lessor notice of intent to extend, ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or any Extended Term, under the terms and rental stipulated to herein
3. Rent
During the Initial Term, Lessee shall pay to Lessor, as rent, the sum of Thirty Six Thousand Dollars ($36,000.00) per year, payable in twelve equal monthly installments of Three-thousand Dollars ($3,000.00) on or before the first day of each month during the term of the Lease.
During any Extended Term provided for herein, the Lessee shall pay rent in amounts as follows: First year: $3,000.00 per month ($36,000.00 annually)
Second year: $3,000.00 per month ($36,000.00 annually)
A late fee of 5% of the monthly rental amount will be levied if the rental payment for any month is received more than 10 days after its due date.
4. Use
The leased property may be used for the following legal and permitted purposes and for no other purposes without the express written consent of the Landlord:
Administrative and sales offices and warehousing distribution purposes.
5. Indemnity
Lessor and Lessee shall mutually indemnify and save harmless the other from any and all losses, fines, suits, damages, expenses, claims, demands and actions of any kind resulting from their negligence, breach, or violation or non-performance of any condition hereof.
6. Insurance
Lessee shall, during the entire term of the Lease keep in full force and effect a policy of public liability insurance with respect to the Property and the business operated by Lessee in the leased property and which the limits of general liability shall be in the amount of One million Dollars ($1,00,000.00) combined single limit, naming Lessor as additional insured. Such coverage shall include a broad form general liability endorsement. The policy shall contain a clause that the Lessee will not cancel or change the insurance without first giving the Lessor ten (10) days prior written notice.
Lessor shall during the term hereof, at it's sole expense, provide and keep in force property hazard insurance on the improvements (building) against loss or damage by (including but not exclusively) fire, wind, or flood, and extended coverage, in an amount equal to one hundred percent (100%) of the full insurable value thereof, which insurance shall be placed with an insurance company or companies approved by Lessor and licensed to do business in the State of Tennessee.
The term "full insurable value" shall mean actual replacement value of the improvements (building) exclusive of costs of excavation, foundations and footing below ground level.
Tenant shall maintain fire and extended coverage insurance covering (1) the replacement cost of all alterations, additions, partitions and improvements installed in the leased property by or on behalf of a Tenant Party and (2) the replacement cost of all of Tenant's personal property in the leased property.
Duplicate originals or certificates of insurance of the policies provided shall be furnished by the Lessee to the Lessor and shall contain an agreement by the insurer that such policy or policies shall not be canceled without at least thirty (30) days prior notice to the Lessor and Lessee.
7. Taxes
a. Lessor will be responsible for and pay all Junction City, Geary County, and the State of Kansas real estate property taxes when due.
b. Lessee shall pay all taxes assessed against all personal property located on the premises and shall also pay all privilege, excise and other taxes duly assessed. Lessee shall pay said taxes when due so as to prevent the assessment of any late fees or penalties.
8. Utilities
Lessee shall pay for all water, electricity, and other utilities used on the premises. Lessor shall guarantee that the above utilities are available at the property.
9. Repairs
Lessor shall be solely responsible for replacing and maintaining the roof, foundation, slab, below grade plumbing, and exterior of the building as well as all structural repairs and replacement. Lessor shall also be responsible for the replacement of any HVAC systems, however, Lessee shall be responsible for maintaining such HVAC systems. Also, Lessee shall be responsible for maintaining the all parking areas and exterior grounds, including snow removal, in good repair for their intended use in the event Lessee damages these areas as a result of Lessee's use.
All other repairs, maintenance, replacement or reconstruction to the interior of the portion of the building leased by Lessee, including but not limited to replacement of glass doors and windows and repair of the electrical, and above ground plumbing, are to be made by Lessee at Lessee's expense.
If Lessee fails to make such repairs or replacements promptly or within thirty (30) days of occurrence, Lessor may, at its option, make such repairs or replacements and Lessee shall repay the costs thereof to Lessor on demand.
Building and all mechanical, plumbing, and electrical systems shall be delivered in good working order upon the commencement of the Lease.
10. Alterations
Lessee shall have the right to make alterations and improvements to the leased property as it may desire and deem necessary if the Lessee first gives written notice of intent to Lessor and then receives Lessor's written approval to make such alterations or improvements. Approval shall not be unreasonably withheld for any alterations requested and Lessor's approval must be received within 10 business days from when the request was sent.
Such alterations and improvements shall comply with all applicable construction laws and regulations and shall conform generally with the existing improvements on the leased property. The Lessee shall keep the leased property free from any and all liens arising out of the work performed or materials furnished in making such improvements.
11. Maintenance
Lessee will keep the leased property in a clean condition and will comply at all times with all lawful health and police regulations.
Lessee shall promptly comply with all of the ordinances, rules, and regulations of Junction City, Geary County, and the State of Kansas, or of any other governmental body applicable for said leased property and to all ordinances and requirements enforced by any authorized and official agency of the above, including but not exclusively, board of health, sanitation, waste, or fire departments, or police departments or agencies for the correction, prevention and abatement of nuisances in and about or connected with the leased property because of Lessee's use thereof during the term of this Agreement, all at Lessee's expense.
Lessee shall provide for the removal of its own trash, waste paper, boxes and cartons and shall
not permit any accumulation of such materials. Lessee shall not engage in any act which shall constitute a nuisance.
12. Default
If Lessee shall make default in the payment of the rent, or any part thereof or any other sums due under the terms hereof, when due as herein provided, or in any of the other covenants, agreements, conditions or undertakings herein contained, and such default shall continue for thirty days after notice thereof in writing to Lessee, or if (a) any proceeding under the bankruptcy act of the United States is begun by or against the Lessee, and an order of adjudication, or order approving the petition, be entered in such proceedings, or (b) a receiver or trustee is appointed for substantially all of the Lessee's business or assets, or (c) if Lessee shall make an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (d) if Lessee shall vacate or abandon the leased property, then, and in any such event, it shall be lawful for the Lessor, at his election, to declare the term hereof ended and to re-enter the leased property, and to repossess and enjoy the said premises and any buildings and improvements situated thereon without such a re-entry and repossession working a forfeiture of the rents to be paid and the covenants to be performed by the Lessee during the full term of this agreement.
If any default shall be made in any covenant, agreement, condition, or undertaking which cannot with due diligence be cured within a period of 30 days, and if notice thereof in writing shall have been given to the Lessee, and if the Lessee, prior to the expiration of 30 days from and after the giving of such notice, shall commence to satisfy the cause of such default and shall proceed diligently and with reasonable dispatch to take all steps and do all work required to cure such default, then the Lessor shall not have the right to declare said term ended by reason of such default; provided, however, that the curing of any default in such manner shall not be construed to limit or restrict the right of Lessor to declare the said term ended and enforce all of their rights and remedies hereunder for any other default not so cured.
The foregoing provision for the termination of this lease for any default in any of its covenants shall not operate to exclude or suspend any other remedy of the Lessor for breach of any of said covenants, or for the recovery of said rent for the full term, and in the event of the termination or default in any of the terms of this lease as aforesaid.
13. Lessor Access
Lessee shall permit Lessor and his agents to enter the leased property at all reasonable times during normal working hours with advance notice for any of the following purposes:
(a) to inspect or maintain the building in which the said premises lease property is,
(b) to make repairs to the leased property as the Lessor is obligated or may elect to make, and
(c) to post notices of non-responsibility for alterations or additions or repairs.
14. Property Damage
If the leased property, including improvements thereon, are injured by fire, flood, or other casualty, Lessee shall have the exclusive right and option to either terminate this Lease or reconstruct and/or repair the said damaged improvements and continue this Lease under its terms and conditions as if no such casualty occurred by giving written notice to Lessor of Lessee's
intention to so continue this Lease within thirty (30) days after the date of said damage or casualty. In the event that Lessee so elects to continue this Lease, any insurance proceeds payable as a result of said fire or casualty shall be first applied to pay the reconstruction or repair of said improvements, and any balance of such insurance proceeds after payment of said reconstruction or repair shall be paid to whoever owns the insurance policy under which payment is made.
15. Condemnation
If, during the term of this Lease, the leased property shall be taken or condemned, either in whole or part, by competent authorities for public or quasi-public use, Lessee shall have the option to terminate this Lease as of the date of taking. If Lessee elects not to terminate this Lease, then this Lease shall continue in full force and effect.
16. Parties
It is understood and agreed that the relationship of the parties hereto is strictly that of Lessor and Lessee and that the Lessor has no ownership in the Lessee's enterprise and the Lease shall not be construed as a joint venture or partnership. The Lessee is not and shall not be deemed to be an agent or representative of the Lessor.
All covenants, conditions and agreements and undertakings contained in this Lease shall extend to and be binding on the respective heirs, successors and assigns of the respective parties hereto the same as if they were in every case named and expressed.
17. Default
It is further understood and agreed by and between the Lessor and Lessee that, due to breach or default by either party of any of their obligations hereunder, it shall become necessary for the other party to employ and/or consult with an attorney to give advice, or to enforce or demand any of either party's rights or remedies hereunder, then, and in any such event, the defaulting or breaching party shall pay all attorney fees, court costs and other expenses occasioned by such default(s) or breach(es).
18. Notices
a, All notices to the Lessee will be sent to: The American Bottling Company 5301 Legacy Drive Plano, Texas 75024 Attn: General Counsel's Office
b. All notices to the Lessor will be sent to: Mr. John A. Trygg 6131 Old Milford Road Milford, KS 66514
All notices to be given under this Lease shall be in writing and shall be served personally or sent by United States certified or registered mail, or overnight courier.
19. Tenant Improvements
Lessor, at Lessor's sole cost and expense, shall commence the following improvements upon full execution of this Lease:
(a) Expand existing office to 12' x 12';
(b) Repaint office and restrooms;
(c) Install new flooring in both office and restrooms (if restroom floors can be cleaned to Lessee's satisfaction, no replacement necessary);
(d) Conduct general cleaning throughout
Lessor shall make all reasonable efforts to complete the above improvements before the commencement of this Lease, but in no event later than ten (10) days after commencement.
20. Modification
This Lease contains all of the agreements and conditions made between the parties hereto and may not be modified orally or in any other manner other than by agreement in writing signed by all parties hereto or their respective successors in interest.
21. Enforcement
If any section, paragraph, sentence or portion of this Lease or the application thereof to any party or circumstance shall, to any extent, be or become invalid or illegal, such provision is and shall be null and void, but, to the extent that said null and void provisions do not materially change the overall agreement and intent of this entire Lease, the remainder of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby and each remaining provision of this Lease shall be valid and enforceable to the fullest extent provided by law.
22. Governance
This Lease shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Kansas.
23. Quiet Enjoyment
Provided Lessee has fully performed its obligations under this Lease, Tenant shall peaceably and quietly hold and enjoy the leased property for the term without hindrance from any party claiming by, through, or under Lessor.
24. Signatures
WITNESS, the signatures of the Parties, this the 12 day of Jamaz , 2013.
LESSOR:
LESSEE:

The American Bottling Company
5301 Legacy Drive
Plano, Texas 75024
Attn: General Counsel's Office

Mr. John A. Trygg
6131 Old Milford Road
Milford, KS 66514

Lessor shall make all reasonable efforts to complete the above improvements before the
commencement of this Lease, but in no event later than ten (10) days after commencement.
20. Modification
This Lease contains all of the agreements and conditions made between the parties hereto and
may not be modified orally or in any other manner other than by agreement in writing signed by
all parties hereto or their respective successors in interest.
21. Enforcement
If any section, paragraph, sentence or portion of this Lease or the application thereof to any party
or circumstance shall, to any extent, be or become invalid or illegal, such provision is and shall
be null and void, but, to the extent that said null and void provisions do not materially change the
overall agreement and intent of this entire Lease, the remainder of this Agreement shall not be
affected thereby and each remaining provision of this Lease shall be valid and enforceable to the
fullest extent provided by law.
22. Governance
This Lease shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of
Kansas.
23. Quiet Enjoyment
Provided Lessee has fully performed its obligations under this Lease, Tenant shall peaceably and
quietly hold and enjoy the leased property for the term without hindrance from any party
claiming by, through, or under Lessor.
24. Signatures
WITNESS, the signatures of the Parties, this the
day of
Jamaz

paragraphs.content: REMOVE RECORD / FACILITY (In the case of a Relocation)
LEGAL ENTITY: The American Bottling Company
ACCOUNTING DETAILS FOR NEW OR REVISED LEASE RECORDS
ADDRESS: 6131 Old Milford Rd; Milford KS 66514
Paid February rent internally. Schedule regular rent beginning March 1. Send hard copy "Landlord Letter" to address provided. No e-mail.
THIS COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (the "Lease") made, entered into and executed this 16 day of Sunnay ,2013, by and between
Konza Investments, Inc. a Kansas Corporation (hereinafter called "Lessor") and
Lessor and Lessee hereby contract and agree as follows:
Lessor hereby leases unto Lessee the land and any improvements located thereon located at 2405 N. Jackson Street, Junction City, KS 66441,

said premises being an approximately 2.3+/- acre tract improved with an approximately 18,580+/- square foot metal building configured as offices and warehouse space (hereafter, "the leased property").

The term of this Lease shall be for one (1) year, commencing on February 1, 2013, and expiring at midnight on January 31st, 2014, unless extended as provided for herein ("Initial Term"). This Lease can be extended at the end of the Initial Term for up to two additional one-year terms ("Extended Term") by Lessee giving Lessor notice of intent to extend, ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or any Extended Term, under the terms and rental stipulated to herein

During the Initial Term, Lessee shall pay to Lessor, as rent, the sum of Thirty Six Thousand Dollars ($36,000.00) per year, payable in twelve equal monthly installments of Three-thousand Dollars ($3,000.00) on or before the first day of each month during the term of the Lease.
During any Extended Term provided for herein, the Lessee shall pay rent in amounts as follows: First year: $3,000.00 per month ($36,000.00 annually)
A late fee of 5% of the monthly rental amount will be levied if the rental payment for any month is received more than 10 days after its due date.
The leased property may be used for the following legal and permitted purposes and for no other purposes without the express written consent of the Landlord:
Administrative and sales offices and warehousing distribution purposes.

Lessor and Lessee shall mutually indemnify and save harmless the other from any and all losses, fines, suits, damages, expenses, claims, demands and actions of any kind resulting from their negligence, breach, or violation or non-performance of any condition hereof.

Lessee shall, during the entire term of the Lease keep in full force and effect a policy of public liability insurance with respect to the Property and the business operated by Lessee in the leased property and which the limits of general liability shall be in the amount of One million Dollars ($1,00,000.00) combined single limit, naming Lessor as additional insured. Such coverage shall include a broad form general liability endorsement. The policy shall contain a clause that the Lessee will not cancel or change the insurance without first giving the Lessor ten (10) days prior written notice.

Lessor shall during the term hereof, at it's sole expense, provide and keep in force property hazard insurance on the improvements (building) against loss or damage by (including but not exclusively) fire, wind, or flood, and extended coverage, in an amount equal to one hundred percent (100%) of the full insurable value thereof, which insurance shall be placed with an insurance company or companies approved by Lessor and licensed to do business in the State of Tennessee.
The term "full insurable value" shall mean actual replacement value of the improvements (building) exclusive of costs of excavation, foundations and footing below ground level.

Tenant shall maintain fire and extended coverage insurance covering (1) the replacement cost of all alterations, additions, partitions and improvements installed in the leased property by or on behalf of a Tenant Party and (2) the replacement cost of all of Tenant's personal property in the leased property.

Duplicate originals or certificates of insurance of the policies provided shall be furnished by the Lessee to the Lessor and shall contain an agreement by the insurer that such policy or policies shall not be canceled without at least thirty (30) days prior notice to the Lessor and Lessee.
a. Lessor will be responsible for and pay all Junction City, Geary County, and the State of Kansas real estate property taxes when due.

b. Lessee shall pay all taxes assessed against all personal property located on the premises and shall also pay all privilege, excise and other taxes duly assessed. Lessee shall pay said taxes when due so as to prevent the assessment of any late fees or penalties.
Lessee shall pay for all water, electricity, and other utilities used on the premises. Lessor shall guarantee that the above utilities are available at the property.

Lessor shall be solely responsible for replacing and maintaining the roof, foundation, slab, below grade plumbing, and exterior of the building as well as all structural repairs and replacement. Lessor shall also be responsible for the replacement of any HVAC systems, however, Lessee shall be responsible for maintaining such HVAC systems. Also, Lessee shall be responsible for maintaining the all parking areas and exterior grounds, including snow removal, in good repair for their intended use in the event Lessee damages these areas as a result of Lessee's use.

All other repairs, maintenance, replacement or reconstruction to the interior of the portion of the building leased by Lessee, including but not limited to replacement of glass doors and windows and repair of the electrical, and above ground plumbing, are to be made by Lessee at Lessee's expense.

If Lessee fails to make such repairs or replacements promptly or within thirty (30) days of occurrence, Lessor may, at its option, make such repairs or replacements and Lessee shall repay the costs thereof to Lessor on demand.
Building and all mechanical, plumbing, and electrical systems shall be delivered in good working order upon the commencement of the Lease.

Lessee shall have the right to make alterations and improvements to the leased property as it may desire and deem necessary if the Lessee first gives written notice of intent to Lessor and then receives Lessor's written approval to make such alterations or improvements. Approval shall not be unreasonably withheld for any alterations requested and Lessor's approval must be received within 10 business days from when the request was sent.

Such alterations and improvements shall comply with all applicable construction laws and regulations and shall conform generally with the existing improvements on the leased property. The Lessee shall keep the leased property free from any and all liens arising out of the work performed or materials furnished in making such improvements.
Lessee will keep the leased property in a clean condition and will comply at all times with all lawful health and police regulations.

Lessee shall promptly comply with all of the ordinances, rules, and regulations of Junction City, Geary County, and the State of Kansas, or of any other governmental body applicable for said leased property and to all ordinances and requirements enforced by any authorized and official agency of the above, including but not exclusively, board of health, sanitation, waste, or fire departments, or police departments or agencies for the correction, prevention and abatement of nuisances in and about or connected with the leased property because of Lessee's use thereof during the term of this Agreement, all at Lessee's expense.
Lessee shall provide for the removal of its own trash, waste paper, boxes and cartons and shall
not permit any accumulation of such materials. Lessee shall not engage in any act which shall constitute a nuisance.

If Lessee shall make default in the payment of the rent, or any part thereof or any other sums due under the terms hereof, when due as herein provided, or in any of the other covenants, agreements, conditions or undertakings herein contained, and such default shall continue for thirty days after notice thereof in writing to Lessee, or if (a) any proceeding under the bankruptcy act of the United States is begun by or against the Lessee, and an order of adjudication, or order approving the petition, be entered in such proceedings, or (b) a receiver or trustee is appointed for substantially all of the Lessee's business or assets, or (c) if Lessee shall make an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or (d) if Lessee shall vacate or abandon the leased property, then, and in any such event, it shall be lawful for the Lessor, at his election, to declare the term hereof ended and to re-enter the leased property, and to repossess and enjoy the said premises and any buildings and improvements situated thereon without such a re-entry and repossession working a forfeiture of the rents to be paid and the covenants to be performed by the Lessee during the full term of this agreement.

If any default shall be made in any covenant, agreement, condition, or undertaking which cannot with due diligence be cured within a period of 30 days, and if notice thereof in writing shall have been given to the Lessee, and if the Lessee, prior to the expiration of 30 days from and after the giving of such notice, shall commence to satisfy the cause of such default and shall proceed diligently and with reasonable dispatch to take all steps and do all work required to cure such default, then the Lessor shall not have the right to declare said term ended by reason of such default; provided, however, that the curing of any default in such manner shall not be construed to limit or restrict the right of Lessor to declare the said term ended and enforce all of their rights and remedies hereunder for any other default not so cured.

The foregoing provision for the termination of this lease for any default in any of its covenants shall not operate to exclude or suspend any other remedy of the Lessor for breach of any of said covenants, or for the recovery of said rent for the full term, and in the event of the termination or default in any of the terms of this lease as aforesaid.
Lessee shall permit Lessor and his agents to enter the leased property at all reasonable times during normal working hours with advance notice for any of the following purposes:
(a) to inspect or maintain the building in which the said premises lease property is,
(b) to make repairs to the leased property as the Lessor is obligated or may elect to make, and
(c) to post notices of non-responsibility for alterations or additions or repairs.

If the leased property, including improvements thereon, are injured by fire, flood, or other casualty, Lessee shall have the exclusive right and option to either terminate this Lease or reconstruct and/or repair the said damaged improvements and continue this Lease under its terms and conditions as if no such casualty occurred by giving written notice to Lessor of Lessee's

intention to so continue this Lease within thirty (30) days after the date of said damage or casualty. In the event that Lessee so elects to continue this Lease, any insurance proceeds payable as a result of said fire or casualty shall be first applied to pay the reconstruction or repair of said improvements, and any balance of such insurance proceeds after payment of said reconstruction or repair shall be paid to whoever owns the insurance policy under which payment is made.

If, during the term of this Lease, the leased property shall be taken or condemned, either in whole or part, by competent authorities for public or quasi-public use, Lessee shall have the option to terminate this Lease as of the date of taking. If Lessee elects not to terminate this Lease, then this Lease shall continue in full force and effect.

It is understood and agreed that the relationship of the parties hereto is strictly that of Lessor and Lessee and that the Lessor has no ownership in the Lessee's enterprise and the Lease shall not be construed as a joint venture or partnership. The Lessee is not and shall not be deemed to be an agent or representative of the Lessor.

All covenants, conditions and agreements and undertakings contained in this Lease shall extend to and be binding on the respective heirs, successors and assigns of the respective parties hereto the same as if they were in every case named and expressed.

It is further understood and agreed by and between the Lessor and Lessee that, due to breach or default by either party of any of their obligations hereunder, it shall become necessary for the other party to employ and/or consult with an attorney to give advice, or to enforce or demand any of either party's rights or remedies hereunder, then, and in any such event, the defaulting or breaching party shall pay all attorney fees, court costs and other expenses occasioned by such default(s) or breach(es).
a, All notices to the Lessee will be sent to: The American Bottling Company 5301 Legacy Drive Plano, Texas 75024 Attn: General Counsel's Office
b. All notices to the Lessor will be sent to: Mr. John A. Trygg 6131 Old Milford Road Milford, KS 66514
All notices to be given under this Lease shall be in writing and shall be served personally or sent by United States certified or registered mail, or overnight courier.
Lessor, at Lessor's sole cost and expense, shall commence the following improvements upon full execution of this Lease:
(c) Install new flooring in both office and restrooms (if restroom floors can be cleaned to Lessee's satisfaction, no replacement necessary);
Lessor shall make all reasonable efforts to complete the above improvements before the commencement of this Lease, but in no event later than ten (10) days after commencement.

This Lease contains all of the agreements and conditions made between the parties hereto and may not be modified orally or in any other manner other than by agreement in writing signed by all parties hereto or their respective successors in interest.

If any section, paragraph, sentence or portion of this Lease or the application thereof to any party or circumstance shall, to any extent, be or become invalid or illegal, such provision is and shall be null and void, but, to the extent that said null and void provisions do not materially change the overall agreement and intent of this entire Lease, the remainder of this Agreement shall not be affected thereby and each remaining provision of this Lease shall be valid and enforceable to the fullest extent provided by law.
This Lease shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Kansas.

Provided Lessee has fully performed its obligations under this Lease, Tenant shall peaceably and quietly hold and enjoy the leased property for the term without hindrance from any party claiming by, through, or under Lessor.
WITNESS, the signatures of the Parties, this the 12 day of Jamaz , 2013.
COMMERCIAL BUILDING FOR SALE or FOR LEASE
